reproducible_artifact: Pre-architecture-review preparation checklist + post-meeting attention-audit. Concrete artifact: a `meeting-prep.md` template that requires each attendee to record the technical-substrate of the proposal before walking in, plus a post-meeting review counting the ratio of substrate-comments to surface-comments per attendee.
embodiment_sketch:
Before each architecture review:
```markdown
## Pre-meeting prep (attendee: <name>)
- Appendix dashboards reviewed: yes/no
- Top 3 technical concerns with the proposal:
  1. <e.g. partitioning hot-spot under projected load>
  2. <e.g. replica-lag SLO mismatch>
  3. <e.g. backfill cost not estimated>
- Question I will ask: <one prepared substrate-question>
```
Post-meeting (3-5 minutes after, before the next meeting):
```markdown
## Attention audit (attendee: <name>)
- Comments I made in the room (count by type):
  - substrate (technical truth of proposal): <n>
  - surface (status/political weather): <n>
- One thing I said that loaded with surface I'd retract: <or none>
```
Falsification: across one quarter, count the substrate:surface ratio per engineer per review. The verse predicts that the engineer who arrives prepared with the appendix-loaded perception produces substrate:surface ratios significantly higher than the unprepared. Track also: did the team's architectural decisions in the quarter correlate with which attendees had high substrate:surface ratios? If decisions correlate with surface comments rather than substrate comments, the meetings are status performances and the architecture decisions are made elsewhere — the verse is then inverted in that organisation, and the audit is the falsification. Pass.
